commencements.subject: Power to disapply requirement for development consent
commencements.description: Amends Planning Act 2008 to add a new subsection (2) in section 31 allowing direction to disapply development consent.

commencements.subject: Applications for development consent: removal of certain pre-application requirements
commencements.description: Removes pre-application consultation duties and related provisions from the Planning Act 2008.

commencements.subject: Applications for development consent: changes related to section 5
commencements.description: Amends the Planning Act 2008 to omit certain provisions in section 37 and amend section 39.

commencements.subject: Applications for development consent: acceptance stage
commencements.description: Amends Planning Act 2008 s.55 to add a new acceptance condition and omit a paragraph.

commencements.subject: Applications for development consent: local impact reports and representations
commencements.description: Amends the Planning Act 2008 to add duties and an exception for local impact reports.

commencements.subject: Examination of applications for development consent
commencements.description: Amends Planning Act 2008 to insert a reference to an assessment and add transitional rule-making power.

commencements.subject: Planning Act 2008: right to enter and survey land
commencements.description: Amends Planning Act 2008 to redefine who may enter and survey land.

commencements.subject: Deadlines for consultation and decisions on certain orders and schemes
commencements.description: Replaces the 6-week consultation deadline under Highways Act 1980 with a minimum period.

commencements.subject: Replacement of model clauses with guidance
commencements.description: In the Transport and Works Act 1992, replaces section 8 (model clauses) with new guidance provisions.

commencements.subject: Delegation of planning decisions in England
commencements.description: Inserts new section 319ZZC into TCPA 1990 giving Secretary of State power to require committee/officer discharge.

commencements.subject: Fees for applications for harbour orders
commencements.description: Amends Schedule 3 to the Harbours Act 1964 regarding application requirements for harbour orders.

commencements.subject: Directions giving deemed planning permission: special regard to heritage assets
commencements.description: This provision extends the special regard duty for listed buildings to government departments under deemed planning permissions.

commencements.subject: Spatial development strategies
commencements.description: Inserts Part 1A into the Planning and Compulsory Purchase Act 2004 requiring spatial development strategies.

affected_pages.title: First-tier Tribunal (Property Chamber)
affected_pages.description: We handle applications, appeals and references relating to disputes over property and land. Residential property disputes that we handle include: rent increases for ‘fair’ or ‘market’ rates leasehold disputes leasehold enfranchisement disputes about park homes improvement notices and prohibition orders where your notice is under the Housing Act 2004 disputes about licences for houses in multiple occupation the right to buy your council home being refused because it’s deemed suitable for elderly people applications under the Electronic Communications Code applications for rent repayment orders appeals against financial penalties imposed by local housing authorities applications under the Building Safety Act Land registration matters that we handle include: disputes over a change to the land register applications to correct or cancel certain documents relating to registered land Agricultural land and drainage matters that we handle include: disputes between agricultural tenants and landlords in relation to certain farming tenancies applications in respect of certain drainage disputes between neighbours. We also handle applications from valuation officers to enter premises to carry out certain valuations. Who we are We are one of 7 chambers of the First-tier Tribunal which settles legal disputes and is structured around particular areas of law. Tribunal information Land Registration hearing list Residential Property hearing list Agricultural Lands and Drainage forms and further guidance Land Registration forms and further guidance Land Registration: a short guide for users Residential Property forms and further guidance procedure rules practice directions and statements - residential property practice directions and statements - land registration previous decisions - residential property previous decisions - land registration complaints procedure
topics: Exclusion of review of Upper Tribunal’s permission-to-appeal decisions
Practice directions relating to mediation
Proceedings in the First-tier Tribunal and Upper Tribunal

affected_pages.title: Administrative Court
affected_pages.description: We review decisions made by people or bodies with a public law function, eg local authorities and regulatory bodies. We can: carry out a judicial review of decisions made by other courts, tribunals and public bodies hear challenges to decisions made by certain people or bodies (eg ministers or local government) where legislation has given the right to challenge We also hear: applications for ‘habeas corpus’, (a legal procedure where the court decides to rule on whether the detention of an individual is legal applications to prevent someone who continues to initiate groundless legal proceedings (a ‘vexatious litigant’) from continuing to do so without first obtaining permission from a court all applications under the Coroners Act 1988 (which deals with the appointment and conduct of coroners) appeals ‘by way of case stated’ from the Crown Court or magistrates’ courts (where our opinion is sought on a particular point of law where a mistake may have been made) applications for an order to imprison a person for contempt of court appeals under the Extradition Act 2003 (which deals with extradition requests to and from the United Kingdom and bail) appeals against decisions made by some professional bodies, eg the Nursing and Midwifery Council applications for ‘restraint orders’ or ‘certificates of inadequacy’ where assets have been frozen or confiscated We also contain a specialist Planning Court which handles judicial reviews of decisions about planning permission and other challenges to planning decisions. Who we are We are a specialist court within the King’s Bench Division of the High Court of Justice. We are based at the Royal Courts of Justice, London and also at centres in Birmingham, Cardiff, Leeds and Manchester. Cases may be heard by one High Court judge or by a ‘Divisional Court’ which consists of 2 or more judges, normally a High Court Judge and a Lord Justice of Appeal. Court information Forms and guidance Judicial policies and practice directions Civil Procedure Rules , Part 54 Daily cause lists RCJ and Rolls Building daily court lists Case references used by the Royal Courts of Justice and Rolls Building User group meeting minutes Previous decisions Fees Extradition
topics: Judicial Review and Courts Act 2022

affected_pages.title: Planning Court
affected_pages.description: We carry out judicial reviews of decisions made by planning authorities, and hear other challenges to planning decisions. This includes appeals and applications relating to: planning permission development consent compulsory purchase orders highways and other rights of way decisions under EU environmental legislation Who we are We are part of the Administrative Court, a specialist court within the King’s Bench Division of the High Court of Justice. We are based at the Royal Courts of Justice in London and at district registries across England and Wales. Cases at the Royal Courts of Justice are heard by the Planning Liaison judge or a High Court judge while cases at district registries are heard by a district judge. Court information forms upcoming hearings Civil Procedure Rules part 54 previous decisions
topics: Judicial Review and Courts Act 2022

affected_pages.title: Upper Tribunal (Immigration and Asylum Chamber)
affected_pages.description: We’re responsible for handling appeals against decisions made by the First-tier Tribunal (Immigration and Asylum) relating to visa applications, asylum applications and the right to enter or stay in the UK. We also handle applications for judicial review of certain decisions made by the Home Office, normally relating to immigration, asylum and human rights claims. Who we are We are one of 4 chambers of the Upper Tribunal which settles legal disputes and is structured around particular areas of law.
